steps.action: Create temp directory with fresh SQLite DB from schema.sql
Insert 3 test messages and generate fake 384-dim normalized embeddings
steps.expected: Messages and embeddings stored in DB

steps.action: Call update_faiss_index() with message IDs and vectors
steps.observed: Both files created: index 4653 bytes, ids 9 bytes

steps.action: Read back FAISS index and verify contents
steps.observed: FAISS index contains 3 vectors, dim=384; IDs [1, 2, 3]

steps.expected: Checks index existence then loads and queries FAISS
steps.observed: Checks FAISS_INDEX_PATH.exists(), loads with faiss.read_index(), queries with index.search()

steps.action: Verify fallback only when _search_faiss returns None
steps.expected: Brute-force only if results is None

codePatterns: Falls back to _search_bruteforce only when _search_faiss returns None

steps.expected: Falls back to brute-force when FAISS unavailable
Returns None when faiss not installed or index missing
steps.observed: Returns None when: _get_faiss() is None, index files don't exist, index.ntotal == 0, or load fails

codePatterns: _search_faiss returns None when faiss module unavailable
_search_faiss returns None when FAISS_INDEX_PATH or FAISS_IDS_PATH don't exist
search_similar checks: if results is None: results = _search_bruteforce(...)

steps.action: Verify rebuild_index subcommand exists in argparse
steps.observed: python src/embed.py rebuild_index --help shows help output

steps.action: Create temp DB with schema, insert messages and embeddings
steps.observed: 3 messages with 384-dim embeddings in temp DB

steps.expected: FAISS index rebuilt from all embeddings
Applies decay then re-sorts and truncates

codePatterns: _search_faiss: fetch_k = min(top_k * 3, index.ntotal) — over-fetches 3x for reranking
search_similar: applies _apply_temporal_decay, sorts by score descending, truncates to top_k
Pattern confirmed: retrieve top_k*3 → apply temporal decay → return top_k

steps.observed: _get_faiss uses try: import faiss; return faiss; except ImportError: return None

steps.action: Test brute-force fallback works when FAISS unavailable
Test update_faiss_index silently returns when faiss unavailable
steps.observed: No FAISS files created, function returned silently

steps.action: Test rebuild_faiss_index reports gracefully when faiss unavailable
steps.observed: Prints 'faiss-cpu is not installed' to stderr, returns 0

codePatterns: _get_faiss(): try: import faiss; return faiss; except ImportError: return None
All FAISS functions call _get_faiss() and handle None return gracefully
update_faiss_index: early return if faiss is None
rebuild_faiss_index: prints error message and returns 0 if faiss is None

summary: Tested 6 assertions: all 6 passed. FAISS index creation (VAL-FAISS-001), FAISS-first search path (VAL-FAISS-002), brute-force fallback (VAL-FAISS-003), rebuild_index subcommand (VAL-FAISS-004), temporal decay reranking with top_k*3 over-fetch (VAL-FAISS-005), and graceful fallback when faiss-cpu not installed (VAL-FAISS-006) all verified through a combination of code inspection and functional testing with temporary databases.
